fab_section_md:
- King Aegon II, Second of His Name, proclaimed victory on Dragonstone after Sunfyre devoured Rhaenyra (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:17)
- He returned to King's Landing aboard the cog Mouse in the waning days of 130 AC and, unable to climb the Iron Throne, held court from a cushioned wooden seat at its base (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:93)
- He judged and destroyed the three "dayfly kings," burned the Shepherd with his own hand, and pursued vengeance on the crownlands (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:113)
- He was found dead of poison in his litter, having lived four-and-twenty years and reigned two (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:279)
- According to Mushroom, his burns had rendered him impotent, though he still watched his favorites couple from behind a curtain and wept afterward (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:187)
identity_line: Aegon II Targaryen — King Aegon II, Second of His Name, proclaimed victory on Dragonstone after Sunfyre devoured Rhaenyra.

fab_section_md:
- Larys Strong the Clubfoot advised the squire Trystane to parley and was sent as envoy across the Blackwater (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:41)
- He brokered the accord in Borros's tent and was betrothed to Floris Baratheon (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:49)
- His honeyed words swayed king and council, and he calmed Alicent into accepting the Sea Snake's terms (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:183)
- Secretly he went to Corlys and revealed the king's plan to grant his demands then murder him (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:183)
identity_line: Larys Strong the Clubfoot advised the squire Trystane to parley and was sent as envoy across the Blackwater.

fab_section_md:
- The Dowager Queen Alicent went with Larys and Orwyle to parley with Borros, and wept with joy at news that her granddaughter Jaehaera was safe at Storm's End (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:45)
- She wanted the Sea Snake dead and was outraged by his demands, Munkun tells us, having lost two of three sons and her only daughter in the Dance (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:83)
- She ruled as proxy, proclaiming a curfew in her son's name and pardoning Corlys (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:77)
- She urged her son to vengeance and to mutilate his hostage Prince Aegon (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:243)
- She was arrested by Velaryon men and returned to the dungeons as her last son died (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:265)
identity_line: Alicent Hightower — The Dowager Queen Alicent went with Larys and Orwyle to parley with Borros, and wept with joy at news that her granddaughter Jaehaera was safe at Storm's End.

fab_section_md:
- Borros Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End, left his pregnant wife and four daughters to march north on hearing of the queen's death (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:37)
- He received the envoys, agreed to wed his daughters to king and Clubfoot, then retook King's Landing and put down the two remaining kings (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:49)
- In council he scorned reconciliation and urged war (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:147)
- He led the doomed charge at the Muddy Mess, was unhorsed, and slew a dozen knights and the Lords Mallister and Darry before Kermit Tully killed him (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:225)
- Seven days later his widow bore his heir, naming him Royce rather than Aegon (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:293)
identity_line: Borros Baratheon, Lord of Storm's End, left his pregnant wife and four daughters to march north on hearing of the queen's death.

fab_section_md:
- Trystane was a squire who styled himself King Trystane Truefyre and held the battlements with Larys and Perkin (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:41)
- Taken and chained when the stormlanders came, he was sentenced to die for high treason (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:97)
- Marston Waters dubbed him Ser Trystane Fyre and Alfred Broome beheaded him with Blackfyre (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:101)
identity_line: Trystane Truefyre — Trystane was a squire who styled himself King Trystane Truefyre and held the battlements with Larys and Perkin.

fab_section_md:
- Gaemon Palehair, the "Cunny King," was a boy who had just turned five and was routed on Visenya's Hill (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:71)
- Spared for his youth, he was made a ward of the Crown (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:103)
- His mother Essie confessed under torture that his father was not the king but a Lyseni oarsman (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:103)
identity_line: Gaemon Palehair, the "Cunny King," was a boy who had just turned five and was routed on Visenya's Hill.

fab_section_md:
- The Shepherd, a one-handed prophet, called his "barefoot army" to defend the Hill of Rhaenys but few obeyed (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:73)
- Taken alive in the ruined Dragonpit, he cursed Borros with words he later repeated to the king (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:73)
- For his insolence Aegon had his tongue torn out with hot pincers, then burned him among the dragon skulls with 241 of his followers (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:109)
identity_line: Shepherd — The Shepherd, a one-handed prophet, called his "barefoot army" to defend the Hill of Rhaenys but few obeyed.

fab_section_md:
- Ser Perkin the Flea, a gutter knight, had raised the squire Trystane as king but abandoned him to join the stormlanders (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:49)
- He reported the pretender taken, was given command of the reformed City Watch, and later turned coat again in the coup (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:253)
- He barred Alfred Broome from Prince Aegon and shoved him to his death on the drawbridge spikes (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:253)
identity_line: Perkin the Flea — Ser Perkin the Flea, a gutter knight, had raised the squire Trystane as king but abandoned him to join the stormlanders.

fab_section_md:
- Alyn of Hull, younger son of Mouse and brother of the dead Addam, took command of the Velaryon fleets at fifteen (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:25)
- His allegiance uncertain, he was courted with pardons then threatened with Baela's execution, and he gathered ships to attack Dragonstone (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:87)
- The Sea Snake's heir, he sent the king home aboard the humble cog Mouse — a deliberate slight, Mushroom claims (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:89)
identity_line: Alyn Velaryon — Alyn of Hull, younger son of Mouse and brother of the dead Addam, took command of the Velaryon fleets at fifteen.

fab_section_md:
- Lady Baela Targaryen, Aegon II's cousin and Rhaena's twin, was held in the Dragonstone dungeons (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:33)
- Twice the king ordered her death, staying his hand only when reminded her mother was a Velaryon, the Sea Snake's daughter (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:33)
identity_line: Baela Targaryen — Lady Baela Targaryen, Aegon II's cousin and Rhaena's twin, was held in the Dragonstone dungeons.

fab_section_md:
- Ser Kermit Tully became Lord of Riverrun on his father Elmo's death, a wild and headstrong youth (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:205)
- At the Muddy Mess he called on Borros to yield and, when the lord charged, killed him with his morningstar (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:225)
identity_line: Kermit Tully — Ser Kermit Tully became Lord of Riverrun on his father Elmo's death, a wild and headstrong youth.

fab_section_md: - He died on the march after only nine-and-forty days as lord (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:205)
- His flanking charge from the wood broke the battle and earned him the name Bloody Ben (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:215)

- Ser Tyland Lannister, the blind master of coin, had placed three-quarters of the Crown's wealth beyond Rhaenyra's reach (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:149)
- He urged hiring sellswords and, more harshly, the immediate execution of Prince Aegon (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:163)
- He was dispatched across the narrow sea to seek sellswords and had not returned (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:231)
identity_line: Tyland Lannister — Ser Tyland Lannister, the blind master of coin, had placed three-quarters of the Crown's wealth beyond Rhaenyra's reach.

fab_section_md:
- The king refused to let him wed Jaehaera or father children, decreeing his line must end (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:161)
- Freed in the coup, he was proclaimed king on his uncle's death and wed Jaehaera in 131 AC, beginning his melancholy reign (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:291)
identity_line: Aegon III Targaryen — The king refused to let him wed Jaehaera or father children, decreeing his line must end.

fab_section_md:
- Ser Alfred Broome had served Aegon II on Dragonstone and beheaded Trystane with Blackfyre (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:101)
- Sent to mutilate Prince Aegon, he was barred by Perkin and shoved off the drawbridge to die on the spikes below (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:251)
identity_line: Alfred Broome — Ser Alfred Broome had served Aegon II on Dragonstone and beheaded Trystane with Blackfyre.

fab_section_md:
- Sunfyre, the golden dragon that was Aegon II's glory, had feasted on Rhaenyra (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:17)
- His neck wounds festered and stank, and he died in the outer yard of Dragonstone on the ninth day of the twelfth moon of 130 AC (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:33)
identity_line: Sunfyre, the golden dragon that was Aegon II's glory, had feasted on Rhaenyra.

fab_section_md: - Marilda of Hull, called Mouse, was the shipwright's daughter and mother of Addam and Alyn (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:25)
identity_line: Marilda of Hull, called Mouse, was the shipwright's daughter and mother of Addam and Alyn.

fab_section_md: - Under torture she recanted her claim that the king was Gaemon's father, and was hanged with Sylvenna Sand and twenty-seven others (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:103)
identity_line: Essie — Under torture she recanted her claim that the king was Gaemon's father, and was hanged with Sylvenna Sand and twenty-seven others.

fab_section_md: - Mushroom says it was apoplexy from his house fighting the king at Second Tumbleton (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:123)
identity_line: Grover Tully — Mushroom says it was apoplexy from his house fighting the king at Second Tumbleton.

fab_section_md:
- Ser Gyles Belgrave of the Kingsguard commanded the king's escort and testified to his grey and ashen look (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:267)
- He found the king dead in the litter with blood on his lips (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:275)
identity_line: Gyles Belgrave — Ser Gyles Belgrave of the Kingsguard commanded the king's escort and testified to his grey and ashen look.

fab_section_md: - Found in a tavern on Eel Alley protesting he was a simple fisherman, his captors drowned him in a cask of ale (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:257)
identity_line: Tom Tanglebeard — Found in a tavern on Eel Alley protesting he was a simple fisherman, his captors drowned him in a cask of ale.

fab_section_md: - Ummet, the king's grossly fat food taster, was murdered in the coup according to Mushroom (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:259)
identity_line: Ummet, the king's grossly fat food taster, was murdered in the coup according to Mushroom.

fab_section_md: - Ser Willis Fell of the Kingsguard delivered Princess Jaehaera safely to Storm's End (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:45)
identity_line: Willis Fell — Ser Willis Fell of the Kingsguard delivered Princess Jaehaera safely to Storm's End.

fab_section_md: - Its size is disputed: Septon Eustace names twenty thousand while Munkun lowers it to eight thousand (fab-short-sad-reign-of-aegon-ii-19:199)
